new_value: Built unified 280-task manifest (schema v2.0). comprehension=100, implementation=90, quality=90. Overall power=84.1% at sigma=0.20. Large codebase 58.6%, multi-repo 31.8%, 20 suites, 11 languages. LOC fallback chain eliminates all unknowns.
Epic complete: (1) IR scoring added to SDLC tasks (ggy), (2) 67 Org tasks got deterministic verifiers (c17), (3) unified 280-task manifest built (utv). No more SDLC/Org split.
Done

new_value:
{"description":"Goal
The canonical 275-task benchmark should expose a consistent hybrid evaluation contract: every task keeps its deterministic verifier, and tasks that support artifact evaluation must map answer.json into the same reporting model rather than creating incomparable ad hoc reward semantics.

Why this exists
The current canonical set is not uniformly dual-mode. A local audit of configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json found:
- 275 canonical tasks total
- 233/275 have Dockerfile.artifact_only
- 144/275 also have Dockerfile.artifact_baseline
- 89/275 include answer_json_verifier_lib.sh
- 136/275 explicitly instruct agents to write answer.json
- 42/275 canonical tasks currently lack artifact_only support entirely

Current shape
- Org suites are close to dual-mode: all selected org tasks have artifact_only and artifact_baseline variants.
- SDLC suites are mixed: deterministic verifier coverage is effectively universal, but answer.json/artifact support is partial and family-specific.
- Reward semantics vary by verifier family (oracle checks, checklist, grep/heuristic repo verification, F1 hybrids, test-ratio), and many reports currently collapse that into a single scalar.

Desired end state
- Canonical benchmark policy explicitly supports a hybrid of deterministic verifier reward and answer.json-derived reward.
- Every canonical task declares evaluator family, expected output artifact(s), and reward interpretation.
- Every canonical verifier emits a standard validation_result payload in addition to reward.txt.
- Reports distinguish continuous reward from pass/solved status and avoid treating unlike scorer families as directly comparable without calibration.
- Remaining missing artifact-mode support is either implemented or intentionally excluded with documented rationale.

Suggested child workstreams
- Audit and classify the 275 canonical tasks by evaluator family and output contract.
- Define the common validation_result schema and pass-threshold semantics.
- Close artifact-mode coverage gaps for canonical tasks, or mark intentional exceptions.
- Update report generation to surface scorer family, pass/fail, and sub-scores.
- Document the hybrid evaluation policy in docs/reference and ops docs.
"}

Every canonical task emits a standard validation payload in addition to /logs/verifier/reward.txt, including scorer family, sub-scores, pass_threshold, passed, and error/failure context. 3. The canonical benchmark defines an explicit hybrid evaluation policy covering deterministic verifier reward and answer.json-derived reward, with family-specific scoring documented and reportable. 4. All 275 canonical tasks are audited for artifact-mode support; gaps are either fixed or tracked as child beads with clear remediation plans. 5. Reporting distinguishes continuous reward from solved/pass status and does not treat different scorer families as directly comparable without calibration.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"sjarmak@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:53:14Z","created_by":"sjarmak","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:53:14Z"}

{"notes":"Canonical evaluation audit landed in configs/canonical_evaluation_audit.json, generated by scripts/audit_canonical_evaluation_contract.py.

Headline counts from the checked-in audit:
- 275 tasks audited (264 active, 11 excluded)
- answer_json modes: 136 native, 89 bridge, 50 none
- artifact primary outputs among artifact-capable tasks: 217 /workspace/answer.json, 8 /app/solution.json, 7 repo-state only, 1 unspecified
- 42 tasks missing Dockerfile.artifact_only
- 16 artifact-capable tasks do not actually use answer.json
- 217 tasks do not yet emit validation_result-style structured verifier output

This confirms the canonical benchmark is hybrid at the benchmark level, but not uniformly dual-mode or uniformly structured-output across all 275 tasks."}

Bring the canonical set closer to the intended hybrid evaluation model by closing answer.json/artifact support gaps where feasible.

- Reuse answer_json_verifier_lib.sh when it fits; avoid bespoke one-offs.
- For tasks that should remain deterministic-only, document the reason explicitly.

Scope:
- Treat active harnesses separately from the full registry-wide check when Gemini is not in scope.

- Confirm the previously failed rerun workflow can be gated without requiring unrelated harness credentials.

This is separate from task-contract migration work and separate from the historical rerun execution/classification task already tracked in Beads.

new_value: All harnesses pass readiness checks. SG token confirmed from .env.local. Gemini excluded from immediate gate.

All 264 active tasks now emit validation_result.json (v1alpha1). 50 tasks migrated across 6 families: ir_checklist(17), checklist(16), f1_hybrid(7), continuous(5), test_ratio(3), f1(2). Commit be8bff87f.

{"notes":"2026-03-09 triage of zero-reward sentinels:

1. element-web-unread-indicators-diverge-fix-001 (Claude MCP, reward=0.0):
   ROOT CAUSE: Task setup bug in sg_only mode. The sgonly_verifier_wrapper restores /repo_full/ but does NOT re-run before_repo_set_cmd (git checkout of specific test files from a different commit). The test patch for threads.ts:137 expects `thread.addEvents(events, true)` but the restored tree has different context. Test patch fails → 0 tests run → reward=0.0. The agent actually implemented the fix correctly. NOT a harness bug — task-specific sg_only incompatibility for SWE-bench Pro tasks that rely on before_repo_set_cmd + test_patch.
   FIX: Either (a) make sgonly_verifier_wrapper run before_repo_set_cmd after restore, or (b) mark this task as sg_only-incompatible and only run in baseline mode.

2. ccx-onboard-search-212 (OpenHands, reward=0.0):
   ROOT CAUSE TWO-PHASE:
   - Phase 1 (trials 1-5): Dockerfile cloned pandas repo into /workspace/, shadowing pip-installed pandas → ModuleNotFoundError. ALREADY FIXED in commit c0c381ba0 (moved WORKDIR to /app).
   - Phase 2 (trials 6-10, post-fix): Agent setup works, agent runs, but produces incorrect answer. This is a LEGITIMATE AGENT FAILURE — the harness works correctly, OpenHands just doesn't solve this semantic retrieval task.
   VERDICT: Harness is clean. No action needed.

Overall sentinel assessment: 7/8 Claude tasks valid (1 is sg_only task bug), OpenHands harness works (agent just fails the task). No general harness regressions."}

{"notes":"2026-03-09 OpenHands verification results — SYSTEMATIC FAILURE:

All 17/18 completed tasks show the same error pattern:
- OpenHands LocalRuntime fails to start: tenacity.RetryError during _wait_until_alive
- Error location: openhands/runtime/impl/local/local_runtime.py:393
- The action execution server (jupyter-kernelgateway + ipykernel) cannot bind/connect
- Agent never actually executes any actions → no output files → verifier scores 0.0
- OpenHands version: 1.4.0

2 false-positive non-zero scores:
- element-web MCP (1.0): Tests passed on pre-existing code because agent made no changes — verifier scored the unmodified state which happened to pass some tests
- django-rate-limit (0.05): Same pattern — verifier scored partial on existing repo state

ROOT CAUSE: OpenHands LocalRuntime is incompatible with Daytona sandbox networking. The LocalRuntime expects to bind localhost ports for its action execution server (jupyter-kernelgateway), but Daytona sandbox networking may not support this.

POSSIBLE FIXES:
1. Switch to DockerRuntime inside Daytona (nested Docker) — unlikely to work
2. Configure OpenHands to use a different port/socket binding
3. Run OpenHands tasks on local Docker instead of Daytona
4. Downgrade OpenHands to a version with compatible runtime
5. Debug the specific RuntimeError inside _wait_until_alive

sgonly_verifier_wrapper.sh fix VERIFIED WORKING: before_repo_set_cmd correctly ran on the element-web MCP task (git reset + checkout visible in verifier log).

1/18 task still running (element-web baseline — SWEAP image build on Daytona is slow)."}

{"id":"CodeScaleBench-ki9","title":"Fix OpenHands runtime crash on Daytona + investigate false-positive verifiers","description":"Two intertwined issues discovered during OpenHands verification batch (runs/staging/openhands_sonnet46_20260309_210054):

## Issue 1: OpenHands LocalRuntime crashes on Daytona (ALL tasks)

Every task (17/18 completed) crashes with:
```
tenacity.RetryError in openhands/runtime/impl/local/local_runtime.py:393 _wait_until_alive
```
OpenHands v1.4.0 LocalRuntime tries to start jupyter-kernelgateway + action execution server on localhost. It fails to bind/connect inside Daytona sandboxes. The agent never executes any actions.

Previous successful OpenHands runs (686 results in staging) must have used a different config or environment. Need to determine what changed.

## Issue 2: Verifiers produce false-positive scores when agent makes no changes

element-web-roomheaderbuttons-can-crash-fix-001 MCP scored 1.0 even though the agent crashed and made ZERO code changes. The verifier ran tests against the unmodified repo and some passed. This is a contract violation — verifiers must detect "no agent output" and score 0.0 before running tests.

Similarly, django-rate-limit-design-001 scored 0.05 on both configs despite the agent never running.

Tasks affected: all test_ratio and repo_state_heuristic verifiers that don't have a guard check for "did the agent actually produce output."","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"sjarmak@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:53:24Z","created_by":"sjarmak","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:53:24Z"}
